name: Rioja Alavesa Dolmens Route
name_original: Dólmenes de las tierras bajas del Territorio Histórico de Álava – circular desde Laguardia
description: Cycle a difficult 18.0-mile route from Laguardia through Rioja Alavesa, exploring ancient dolmens and vineyards with mountain views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Laguardia traverse a landscape characterized by rolling vineyards, historic towns, and significant elevation changes. The region, part of Rioja Alavesa, features ancient dolmens and natural lagoons, offering diverse scenery for cyclists. Routes often include climbs into the Sierra de Cantabria, providing panoramic views over the wine-growing plains. The terrain varies from gentle paths through agricultural areas to challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Laguardia The most popular touring cycling route is Rioja Alavesa Dolmens Route, an 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete, passing by ancient megalithic structures. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Marqués de Riscal Hotel – Bodegas Ysios loop from Laguardia, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route connects two prominent wineries, offering views of modern architecture amidst vineyards. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguardia Old Town – Laguardia Lagoons loop from Laguardia, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail leading through the historic town center and natural lagoons, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Laguardia is defined by vineyards, historic villages, and significant climbs into the surrounding hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. The routes in Laguardia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.77 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Laguardia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Laguardia?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Laguardia, offering a wide range of experiences for cyclists. The region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.77 stars from over 19 reviews.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Laguardia?
A: While many routes in Laguardia feature significant elevation changes, there are a few moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguardia Old Town – Laguardia Lagoons loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 19 minutes, offering a less strenuous ride through the historic town and natural lagoons.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced touring cyclists expect in Laguardia?
A: Advanced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes in Laguardia, characterized by significant climbs into the Sierra de Cantabria. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31910917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas Ysios – Puerto de Herrera (1,100 m) loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 39.7 miles (63.9 km) path, feature over 1,200 meters of elevation gain, providing demanding ascents and rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Laguardia?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Laguardia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085184040/rioja-alavesa-dolmens-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alavesa Dolmens Route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguardia Old Town – Laguardia Lagoons loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling near Laguardia?
A: Laguardia's touring cycling routes often pass by beautiful natural features. You can explore several natural lagoons, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Prao de la Paul&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4450860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Carralogroño&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flushing_Meadows%E2%80%93Corona_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Cantabria&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning natural backdrops.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see on a touring cycling route?
A: Absolutely. Laguardia is rich in history and culture. Routes often traverse ancient dolmens, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085184040/rioja-alavesa-dolmens-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alavesa Dolmens Route&lt;/a&gt;. You can also cycle through the historic Laguardia Old Town and past prominent wineries like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20062582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marqués de Riscal Hotel – Bodegas Ysios loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;, which showcase modern architecture amidst vineyards.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Laguardia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Laguardia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.77 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from rolling vineyards and historic towns to the challenging climbs into the Sierra de Cantabria, and the well-maintained paths that offer a unique blend of nature and culture.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Laguardia?
A: The best time to go touring cycling in Laguardia is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild, and the vineyards offer beautiful scenery, either in bloom or with vibrant autumn colors. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, especially in the higher elevations.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Laguardia?
A: While many routes involve significant climbs, some of the moderate, shorter loops through the flatter vineyard areas or around the natural lagoons can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Always check the route's elevation profile and distance before setting out with family.

Q: Can I expect scenic views on touring cycling routes in Laguardia?
A: Yes, touring cycling in Laguardia offers numerous scenic viewpoints. Routes often provide panoramic views over the wine-growing plains of Rioja Alavesa, especially from higher elevations in the Sierra de Cantabria. You'll also enjoy picturesque vistas of historic villages, ancient dolmens, and natural lagoons.

Q: Are there routes that pass by wineries or offer a cultural experience?
A: Laguardia is at the heart of the Rioja Alavesa wine region, so many routes naturally incorporate wineries and cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20062582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marqués de Riscal Hotel – Bodegas Ysios loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, connecting two iconic wineries. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elciego Wineries – IglesiaDeSantaMariaLaMayor loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt; also offers a rich cultural and wine-focused experience.
tips: It is a small dolmen next to a road in very good condition.
Interesting construction in which to replenish fresh water

The Los Llanos dolmen is one of the most interesting prehistoric monuments in Rioja Alavesa, located between Guardia and Kripan, next to the hermitage of San Sebastián. It preserves a corridor and a polygonal burial chamber formed by large slabs, allowing one to clearly imagine its original structure even though the tumulus is covered in vegetation.
Mythical place through a very fun trail from Elvillar

Between 1200 BC and 250 BC this town was the most important in the area we know today as Rioja Alavesa. The town was structured into streets and squares and was the most populated in the area.

In addition to the excavations of the archaeological site, there is a small museum.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This cycling route is rated as difficult. It features significant ascents and descents, making it best suited for experienced cyclists or those using electric bicycles. The terrain includes a mix of paved roads and dirt tracks through vineyards and productive lands.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 29 kilometers (18 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 17 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What historical sites and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in history, featuring several ancient megalithic dolmens. You will pass by the Dolmen El Entrenal, the Dolmen of Los Llanos, and the famous Dolmen of the Witch's Hut. Additionally, you can explore the Medieval Fountain of Viñaspre and the ancient Town of La Hoya.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, encompassing productive lands around the Ebro Valley and extensive vineyards. You can expect a mix of paved roads and dirt tracks, with notable ascents and descents throughout the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this cycling trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation changes, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness or the use of an electric bicycle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and the landscapes of the Rioja Alavesa vineyards are particularly beautiful. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Dólmenes de las tierras bajas cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on public paths in the region, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in towns. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and distance if cycling with a dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route. The trail traverses public lands and roads within the Rioja Alavesa region. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts and ends in Laguardia, you can find parking within or near the historic town. Laguardia offers various public parking options, though availability might vary, especially during peak tourist seasons. It's advisable to arrive early to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Ignatian Way and also crosses parts of the Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea and the Laguardia - Lagrán (GR-38) trails.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists will enjoy impressive landscapes, including panoramic views of the Sierra Cantabria mountains and the picturesque, extensive vineyards of the Rioja Alavesa region. The route offers a unique blend of historical sites and natural beauty.
